title: Agent Client Protocol v2 reaches its first public draft
friendlyTitle: A shared connection standard for coding agents gets a v2 draft
summary: The ACP team has published the first draft of version 2, with focused breaking changes intended to express more agent-session states and make protocol behaviour more consistent. The project says further features will continue through its request-for-discussion process.
friendlySummary: ACP helps coding agents connect to different apps and editors. Its new draft aims to handle more session states in a clearer way.
whyItMatters: A stable protocol can let coding agents work across more editors and clients without every pairing needing a custom integration. Because v2 is still a draft, tool builders should experiment and provide feedback rather than treat the interface as final.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could make it easier to use the same coding agent in different tools, but developers should expect changes while the draft is reviewed.
